message:
test(readme): integration test for Selection and Indexing (fd90.187 / br-sy8p4)

Adds readme_selection_and_indexing_compiles_and_runs covering 7 APIs
documented in README "Selection and Indexing" (lines 1522-1558) that
previously had no integration coverage:

Conditional replacement (all four where/mask permutations):
- where_mask_df (mask + scalar fill)
- where_cond_df (mask + DataFrame fill)
- mask_df (inverse + scalar fill)
- mask_df_other (inverse + DataFrame fill)

Index management:
- set_index (column → index with drop=true)
- reset_index (index → column with drop=false)

Dtype filtering by name:
- select_dtypes_by_name (string-form variant of select_dtypes)

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — only sort_index from
this section was tested (in readme_sorting), the rest had drifted
out of integration coverage.

Verified: 26 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-sy8p4","title":"fd90.187: Integration test for README Selection and Indexing section","description":"fd90.187: Add integration test for README "Selection and Indexing" section (lines 1522-1558).

This section documents conditional replacement (where_mask_df / where_cond_df / mask_df / mask_df_other) and index-management (set_index / reset_index / select_dtypes_by_name) APIs. Only sort_index has integration coverage today; the rest are uncovered by tests/readme_quick_example.rs.

Scope:
- DataFrame.where_mask_df (mask + scalar fill)
- DataFrame.where_cond_df (mask + DataFrame fill)
- DataFrame.mask_df (mask + scalar fill, inverse semantics)
- DataFrame.mask_df_other (mask + DataFrame fill, inverse)
- DataFrame.set_index (column → index, drop=true)
- DataFrame.reset_index (index → column)
- DataFrame.select_dtypes_by_name (string-name variant of select_dtypes)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (one new test, ~50 lines).
Time estimate: 30-40min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:44:08.995470373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:46:06.804322368Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:46:06.802235255Z","close_reason":"Added readme_selection_and_indexing_compiles_and_runs covering 7 conditional/index APIs: where_mask_df, where_cond_df, mask_df, mask_df_other (all four where/mask permutations), set_index (drop=true), reset_index (drop=false), and select_dtypes_by_name (string variant). All 26 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-szafc","title":"fd90.106: README claims df.squeeze(0) returns Scalar but DataFrame::squeeze always returns Series","description":"fd90.74-105 README audit. Lines 1131-1132 in DataFrame Introspection show:

  let series = single_col_df.squeeze(1)?;   // DataFrame → Series
  let scalar = single_cell_df.squeeze(0)?;  // DataFrame → Scalar

The second claim is wrong. crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:24938 declares pub fn squeeze(&self, axis: usize) -> Result<Series, Self> on DataFrame — it always returns Series, never Scalar. Series::squeeze (no args, line 5146) returns Result<Scalar, Box<Self>>. So to get DataFrame → Scalar you need to chain: df.squeeze(0)?.squeeze().

A user copy-pasting line 1132 would bind 'scalar' to a Series, not a Scalar — misleading and would cascade through their code.

Fix: rewrite line 1132 to either chain through Series::squeeze(), or correct the comment to 'DataFrame → Series (then call .squeeze() on the Series for the Scalar)'. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:25:07.187155484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:26:27.185866161Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:26:27.183147337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-szs9","title":"fd90.38: SqlInspector wrapper for unified introspection API","description":"Add SqlInspector<'a, C: SqlConnection> struct that wraps a connection reference and exposes all the fd90.20-37 introspection methods as a single unified API matching SQLAlchemy.Inspector(engine) shape. Methods: tables(schema), views(schema), schemas(), columns(table, schema), indexes(table, schema), foreign_keys(table, schema), unique_constraints(table, schema), primary_key_columns(table, schema), table_comment(table, schema), table_exists(table, schema), server_version(), max_identifier_length(). Pure delegation — no new behavior, just ergonomic unification. Constructor: SqlInspector::new(&conn). Free-fn alias inspect(conn). Tests cover: each method round-trips through to the underlying trait, multi-schema stub propagation, single inspector instance reused across calls.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:59:16.216171484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:02:20.687822783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:02:20.684452315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
